worker_phase.description: Subset of `phase` values a Worker mandate may declare. Workers operate only in the execution-side phases; interpret/parse/load run before any worker is dispatched (interpret at the Will layer, parse/load as deterministic pre-execution stages). Constitutional subset of `phase`. Consumed via $ref by .intent/META/worker.schema.json#/properties/mandate/properties/phase. Loader: src/shared/workers/declaration_validator.py — fails closed on empty or missing.

component_phase.description: Subset of `phase` values a runtime Component may operate in. Excludes `interpret` because the INTERPRET phase runs at the Will/interpreter layer before any context is built and no Component is dispatched into it. Constitutional subset of `phase`. Mirrors src/shared/infrastructure/context/models.py PhaseType Literal and src/shared/infrastructure/intent/task_type_phases.py _VALID_PHASES. Drift between this enum and either Python site is caught by tests/shared/infrastructure/test_phase_enum_canonical_alignment.py.

failure_mode.description: Response strategy a phase invokes when a constitutional failure of a specific class occurs (ADR-074). Consumed via $ref by .intent/META/phase.schema.json#/properties/failure_modes/additionalProperties (values of the failure-class → response-strategy map declared in .intent/phases/*.yaml). `block` halts the workflow (used for contradictions, rule violations, structural illegibility, action denials, mutation failures); `clarify` halts pending user dialogue (used for INTERPRET-phase ambiguity per UR-03). v1 vocabulary is restricted to these two strategies; TBD candidates (`warn`, `continue`, `defer`, `escalate`) are listed in ADR-074 D3 for governor review.

worker_status.description: Operational status of a Worker declaration. active means the daemon loads and runs it. paused means the daemon skips it without retiring it. deprecated means it is retained for reference only and will not be loaded.

blackboard_entry_status.description: Canonical lifecycle states for all Blackboard entries. Three categories. (1) Active and claimable: open, claimed — workers MAY claim and transition these. (2) Active but non-claimable, awaiting sensor adjudication: awaiting_reaudit — workers MUST NOT claim entries in this state; the audit sensor that owns the rule namespace re-evaluates each such entry on its next cycle and transitions it to open (violation still holds) or resolved (cleared). This state exists to gate the remediator's claim queue against stale finding payloads after §7a revival; see ADR-045. (3) Terminal: resolved, abandoned, deferred_to_proposal, dry_run_complete, indeterminate, suppressed — no worker may claim or transition these without explicit revival. abandoned and suppressed differ in re-emission semantics: an abandoned subject MAY be re-emitted by its sensor on a fresh detection (workers gave up but the underlying issue persists); a suppressed subject MUST be skipped permanently by sensors (deliberate governor signal that the subject must not be surfaced again). See issue #263 for the abandoned/suppressed split rationale.

blackboard_entry_status_active.description: The subset of blackboard_entry_status values from which a Worker MAY claim findings. Distinct from 'non-terminal': awaiting_reaudit is also non-terminal but explicitly excluded here, because workers must not act on findings whose truth claim is awaiting sensor re-evaluation (ADR-045). Worker claim queries MUST filter to exactly these values.

proposal_status.description: Canonical lifecycle states for Proposals. draft, pending, approved, and executing are active — deduplication must treat these as blocking a re-creation of the same action group. completed, failed, and rejected are terminal. rejected is the governor-veto outcome: the proposal completed its review path but was explicitly not approved (distinct from failed, which is execution failure of an approved proposal).

proposal_status_active.description: The subset of proposal_status values that are active. RemediatorWorker MUST check for proposals in these states before creating a new one for the same action group.

proposal_risk.description: Risk classification for a Proposal. Determines whether approval_required is true. safe proposals are auto-approved. moderate and high require human review.

proposal_approval_authority.description: Closed set of authority values for the approval_authority column on core.autonomous_proposals. Identifies the role under which a proposal was approved. Non-omittable when status transitions to approved, executing, or completed (per URS NFR.5; URS Q2.A; ADR-015 D6). risk_classification.safe_auto_approval is the autonomous self-promote authority (principal.system), used when compute_risk() classifies the proposal as safe and approval_required is False. principal.governor is the authority for approvals issued by a human Governor. Note: this enum governs only the approval_authority column — it is not the full principal role taxonomy. Roles that carry no proposal approval authority (principal.operator, principal.auditor, principal.system) do not appear here. Full taxonomy: .intent/taxonomies/principal_roles.yaml. Authority: ADR-068 D5.

dry_run_scope.description: Disambiguates the three distinct dry-run modes in CORE. evaluation_only means the rule was checked but no fix was generated. fix_generated means a fix was produced but not applied. finding_suppressed means the finding is tagged for exclusion from remediation pipelines.

blackboard_entry_type.description: Canonical values for BlackboardEntry.entry_type. Governed by ADR-056 D5 (#366). Posted via shared/workers/base.py post_finding/post_report/post_heartbeat helpers. The 'claim' value is reserved for future use; current workers track claims via status='claimed' on existing entries rather than posting a new entry_type='claim'. The 'proposal' value is used by the proposal pipeline.

blackboard_subject.description: Canonical subject vocabulary for BlackboardEntry.subject (ADR-056 #366). Sensors and shop managers join on these strings; drift here breaks coherence detection silently. Two shapes share this enum: (1) literal subjects matched exactly (e.g. `worker.heartbeat`, `<worker_name>.run.complete`); (2) family roots for parametric subjects of the form `<root>::<params>`, matched via `subject LIKE '<root>::%'` (e.g. root `prompt.extraction` represents posted subjects `prompt.extraction::<file_path>::<line_number>`). The closed-enum invariant applies to both shapes (governor decisions 2026-05-23 on #366 D5 residual 1 and #258): when a new worker emits a literal subject, or when a new parametric subject family is introduced, the literal or family root MUST be added to this enum.

action_impact.description: Classification of an atomic action's impact on system state (ADR-056 #366). Mirrors src/shared/action_types.py:25 ActionImpact enum value strings. Used by ActionRegistry impact_level overlay (.intent/enforcement/config/action_risk.yaml) and governance routing.

action_category.description: Constitutional category for atomic actions (ADR-056 #366). Mirrors src/body/atomic/registry.py:41 ActionCategory enum value strings. Used for action-registry indexing and category-level governance rules.

refusal_type.description: Category of constitutional refusal as a first-class outcome (ADR-056 #366). Mirrors src/shared/models/refusal_result.py:79 valid_types set. Persisted into core.refusals.refusal_type.

step_kind.description: Discriminator for FlowStep references (ADR-056 #366). Mirrors src/body/flows/registry.py:28 StepKind enum value strings. 'action' resolves to AtomicAction in ActionRegistry; 'flow' resolves to a nested Flow in FlowRegistry.

task_type.description: Canonical task types from the INTERPRET phase (ADR-056 #366). Mirrors src/will/interpreters/request_interpreter.py:34 TaskType enum value strings. TBD / governor review: a parallel ExecutionTask.task_type vocabulary is governed by ADR-003 via .intent/enforcement/config/task_type_phases.yaml. Open question whether the two should consolidate or remain separate (INTERPRET-phase intent vs execution-routing intent).

audit_severity.description: Severity levels for audit findings. BLOCK halts execution (sole CI-blocking severity); HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW are actionable at decreasing urgency; INFO is informational only. Governed by ADR-059. Python source: src/shared/models/audit_models.py AuditSeverity IntEnum (stored as lowercase string via __str__). CIM CheckResult.severity (src/body/services/cim/models.py) uses the same vocabulary per ADR-059 D2. Translation to log level (per ADR-059 D3 Boundary 2): block/high → ERROR, medium → WARNING, low/info → INFO.

risk_tier.description: Risk classification used by the constitutional validator's input layer (ADR-056 #366). Mirrors src/body/services/constitutional_validator.py:34 RiskTier enum names, stored as lowercase strings. Governor decision (2026-05-18): intentionally separate from 'proposal_risk' (safe/moderate/high). risk_tier is the validator's pre-decision input classification; proposal_risk is the proposal's own self-assessment. Different lifecycle points, different concerns. Note: Python defines RiskTier with integer values (ROUTINE=1, STANDARD=3, ELEVATED=7, CRITICAL=10); the enums.json vocabulary uses the name (lowercased) as the canonical string form.

approval_type.description: Gating mechanism for governance approvals (ADR-056 #366). Mirrors src/body/services/constitutional_validator.py:44 ApprovalType enum value strings. Governor decision (2026-05-18): intentionally separate from 'proposal_approval_authority'. approval_type is the gating mechanism (what kind of approval is required, evaluated pre-decision); proposal_approval_authority is the post-fact record (who/what authority actually approved). Both are necessary at different points in the lifecycle.

fs_audit_op_class.description: Operation-class vocabulary for the audit-time call-name → op-class taxonomy used by .intent/taxonomies/filesystem_operations.yaml. Read-axis decomposition (read/traverse/parse distinguish what kind of read) with write left as a single class and neutral for non-FS calls. Authority: ADR-077 §2, ADR-080. Not the same vocabulary as fs_operation_class — the two surfaces decompose on different axes for different jobs; see ADR-080. Loader: src/shared/infrastructure/intent/filesystem_operations.py reads this enum's values to validate per-call-entry op_class declarations; values outside this enum are a load-time failure.

fs_operation_class.description: Operation-class vocabulary for the write-axis decomposition used by operational_capabilities.yaml per-capability fs_profile keys. Authority: ADR-078. Not the audit-time call-name vocabulary — that lives in fs_audit_op_class (ADR-080), which uses a separate read-axis decomposition for the filesystem_operations.yaml taxonomy declared by ADR-077. The two surfaces' op-class vocabularies are deliberately distinct because they decompose on different axes for different jobs (authorization vs audit completeness). Loader: src/shared/infrastructure/intent/operational_capabilities.py reads this enum's values to derive the expected fs_profile key set; drift between the YAML's keys and this enum is a load-time failure.

operational_mode.description: Process-level mode flag governing capability-scoped filesystem authorization. dev = governor in the loop; live = no governor in the loop. Immutable for the process lifetime per papers/CORE-Capability-Scoped-Filesystem-Authority.md §6. Authority: ADR-078. Mode-flag startup mechanism and provenance guarantees deferred to a separate ADR (#492). Loader: src/shared/infrastructure/intent/operational_capabilities.py reads this enum's values to validate the modes list on each fs_profile pattern entry; values outside this enum are a load-time failure.
